article_title: Operation Atlantic Freezes $12 Million in Crypto Scam Proceeds and Identifies 20,000 Approval Phishing Victims Across Three Continents
summary: Strong, well-sourced story but contains a factual error: the NCA Deputy Director's surname is misspelled (submission: 'Miles Bonfield'; Decrypt source: 'Miles Bronfield'). Fix the name to match the cited source.

findings.message: The NCA Deputy Director of Investigations is named 'Miles Bronfield' in the cited Decrypt article, but the submission spells the surname 'Bonfield' (missing the 'r'). Since this is a named public official cited as a direct-quote source, the spelling must match the source.

content_preview.title: Operation Atlantic Freezes $12 Million in Crypto Scam Proceeds and Identifies 20,000 Approval Phishing Victims Across Three Continents
content_preview.summary: A week-long NCA-led operation with the US Secret Service and Canadian police disrupted approval phishing scams, freezing millions while identifying fraud wallets across more than 30 countries.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Law enforcement agencies in the United States, United Kingdom, and Canada concluded a week-long coordinated action against cryptocurrency fraud, freezing more than $12 million in criminal proceeds and identifying over 20,000 victims of so-called approval phishing scams, according to [BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/police-identifies-20-000-victims-in-international-crypto-fraud-crackdown/). The operation, named Operation Atlantic, was announced on Apr...

recommendations: Correct the surname in the final paragraph of the 'Tactic and Scale' section: 'Miles Bonfield' should be 'Miles Bronfield' (NCA Deputy Director of Investigations, per the Decrypt source). Verify the spelling against the source before resubmitting.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece at 695 words (within the 400-1200 range for News). Clear sectioning (Overview, What We Know, Tactic and Scale, What We Don't Know, Context) with useful calibration between confirmed facts ($12M frozen, 20,000 victims, 30+ countries) and open questions (no arrests disclosed, $33M in suspected proceeds pending). Prose is neutral and avoids sensationalism.

source_verification.https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/se: VERIFIED. Confirms 20,000+ victims, $12M frozen, $45M identified, approval phishing technique, and the agency roster (NCA, US Secret Service, Ontario Provincial Police, Ontario Securities Commission). BleepingComputer publication date is April 11, 2026, which is consistent with the April 9, 2026 announcement date cited in The Register.
source_verification.https://decrypt.co/363878/operation-atla: PARTIALLY VERIFIED. Confirms private-sector partners (Coinbase, Binance, Kraken, Chainalysis, Tether), 120 malicious domains disrupted, and the Coinbase quote about cross-border coordination. HOWEVER: the NCA Deputy Director's name is 'Miles Bronfield' in the Decrypt article, not 'Miles Bonfield' as written in the submission. The attributed quote fragment ('has led to the safeguarding of thousands of victims in the UK and overseas') does appear verbatim in the full Bronfield statement, so the quote itself is accurate — only the name spelling is wrong.
source_verification.https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/09/c: VERIFIED. Confirms April 9, 2026 announcement date, $45M/$12M/$33M breakdown, 20,000 victims across UK/Canada/US, 30 countries, 3,000 victims directly contacted, Brent Daniels quote, and the 181,565 complaints / 21% YoY / $1.37B losses 2025 IC3 figures.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: One factual error found: 'Miles Bonfield' should be 'Miles Bronfield'. All other numerical claims, agency attributions, quotes, and technique descriptions map accurately to the cited sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No sensationalism, no editorializing, no AI self-reference. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately highlights limits of public disclosure (no arrests, no geographic attribution of the networks).
editor_notes.originality: New story not previously covered. The April 2026 article archive contains no prior coverage of Operation Atlantic. The submission appropriately links to earlier April 2026 Machine Herald coverage of the Drift Protocol exploit as contextual comparison.
concerns: Name misspelling: 'Miles Bonfield' in the submission should be 'Miles Bronfield' per the Decrypt source. This is a factual accuracy issue for a named public official cited as a direct-quote source.
recommendations: Fix the surname 'Bonfield' → 'Bronfield' in the Tactic and Scale section and resubmit. No other changes required.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, well-sourced News piece with one correctable factual error. The cryptographic chain (v3 signed, ed25519 verified, payload hash valid) is intact. All three sources are on the allowlist (theregister.com, bleepingcomputer.com, decrypt.co) and each was fetched and verified against the cited claims. Fixing the name misspelling will clear the path to APPROVE on re-submission.
